run.true_intent: Exactly what I said: a daily 9am digest of the top 5 Hacker News stories, delivered to the in-app titlebar. Nothing more — no external publishing, no review step needed, it's just an informational read.
run.vague_intent: Every day at 9am, fetch the top 5 Hacker News stories and show them to me as a digest in the app titlebar. No need to publish anywhere else.

transcript.question: How should "Daily HN Digest" format its output? Paste / attach an example, or pick a shape:
options: Markdown — bullet list with short headings
Prose summary — short paragraphs, no bullets
transcript.answer: Markdown — bullet list with short headings, one line per story.

transcript.question: What should "Daily HN Digest" remember between runs? (Pick "Nothing" if it doesn't need memory.)
options: Items I've already approved or rejected
Recurring context (people, projects, topics I care about)
transcript.answer: Nothing — each run is independent, it just pulls the current top 5 stories each morning.

transcript.question: Should "Daily HN Digest" wait for your approval before publishing its output?
options: Never — auto-publish; I can undo/discard myself
On low confidence — only pause when unsure
Always — I want to sign off every run
transcript.answer: Never — auto-publish; it's just a read-only digest, I can ignore it if it's junk.

transcript.question: Which storage connector should "Daily HN Digest" use?
transcript.answer: No storage — it just posts the digest to the in-app titlebar each morning.

capabilities.title: Daily HN Digest
capabilities.description: Fetch the top Hacker News stories each morning at 9am and auto-publish them as a Markdown bullet-list digest in the app titlebar. No storage, no review gate.

subscriptions.description: Emitted after the daily digest is auto-published to the titlebar, carrying status and story count.
